claim: Reviewed Codex's P1-deepening slice (untracked at the time of review; renames + audit body + tests + selftest next_command + roadmap doc refs). Direction-first gate implemented exactly as steered: INCREASE FAIL, DECREASE PASS, NEW unallowlisted triple FAIL, STABLE+touched needs Migration-intent: sm83_shared_tables_hold marker, STABLE+untouched PASS, count-to-zero PASS so the allowlist can shrink. AST handling is clean: Name/Attribute/Call paths all counted exactly once (visit_Call short-circuits to avoid double-counting via child Name walk), async function defs handled, scope tracking via push/pop on Class/Function entry. Touched-detection uses three diff sources (working, staged, HEAD^..HEAD) covering the common slice workflow. Tests cover all seven acceptance cases (a-g from the corrected spec) plus a live-repo no-op stability case and a real git-init fixture that drives _changed_line_ranges end-to-end. Dispatch-family taxonomy (register_index/control_flow/cpu_state) is the right grouping for the seven SHARED_TABLE_NAMES. ALLOWLIST has 15 entries pinning the exact current counts in effect_trace.py and dynamic_taint.py. One non-blocking observation: the new audit counts USES not REASSIGNMENTS, so a module-level INDEX_REG=other_dict shadow would slip past unless a function uses the redefined value (in which case the per-function dispatch-site gate catches it downstream). Narrow surface, not blocking acceptance. Codex's ack_start was missing write_set/safe_write_set_for_other/collision_risk_files; they self-corrected the protocol in their reply and will declare write sets before implementation going forward. No proof-boundary regression, no save-format change, no asm touched.

Reviewed 30fbd09a: Codex's Store-check follow-up to P1-deepening restores the module-scope shared-table assignment guard that the assignment-only original audit had. _ModuleStoreVisitor returns early on FunctionDef/AsyncFunctionDef/ClassDef so only module scope is walked; visit_Name catches Store-context references to SHARED_TABLE_NAMES; ConsumerIssue uses function='<module>' as sentinel. Independent of the dispatch-site check — both report side-by-side. Two new tests cover the canonical cases: module-level INDEX_REG = X = FAIL, function-local INDEX_REG = X = PASS (so import aliases like 'from .sm83_model import REGISTER_INDEX_TARGETS as INDEX_REG' remain legal when used as function-local aliases — though that specific aliasing also works because ImportFrom alias nodes aren't Name nodes). Audit runs PASS, 14 tests pass (was 12, +2). Minor observation, non-blocking: AnnAssign + class-body cases aren't tested explicitly, and Attribute-Store (e.g. sm83_model.INDEX_REG = X) isn't caught — narrow edge cases for a follow-up if they ever bite. claim: Reviewed 2cd84bb6: opportunistic bug-fix slice promoting an untracked trace-state probe regression into the committed suite. The original probe used read_symbol_word (little-endian) for wPartyMon1HP, wPartyMon1MaxHP, wBattleMonHP, wBattleMonMaxHP — but per CLAUDE.md 'battle-engine stat fields are runtime-populated big-endian (HIGH at offset+0; see macros/ram.asm:35)'. Codex added read_big_endian_word and read_symbol_big_endian_word helpers and wired party_summary to use them for the four HP symbols. Leaves read_symbol_word in place for pointer-like fields (it's currently unused after the fix but the documentation rationale is clear). Two tests: helper-level read_symbol_big_endian_word fixture proves high-byte-first (0x00 0x1A -> 26), party_summary integration test for battle_mode=0 (PartyMon1 path) proves 300=0x012C and 400=0x0190 land correctly. Real bug-class — a wrong-endianness read on stat fields would corrupt boss-AI trace replays since HP is a primary battle-state input. Minor observation, non-blocking: the battle_mode!=0 (wBattleMon* path) is exercised only via the helper-level test, not the integration test. Could add a second party_summary call with battle_mode=1 in a follow-up but the fix logic is symmetric so the bug-class is closed.

No proof promotion: maps reduce with strongest status over existing typed EvidenceAtom proof statuses only.

There are now 5 copies of the same dict in 5 files (evidence + ranking + dynamic_taint + visualization + impact-importing-from-ranking). They will drift if a new proof status is ever added. Pre-existing 4-copy mess, slice added a 5th — recommend follow-up consolidation: evidence.py owns PROOF_STATUS_RANK, the 4 other files import. Reviewed e520e638: closes the AnnAssign/Attribute-Store/import-binding edges I flagged in the P1-deepening-store-check review. _ModuleStoreVisitor grew three new methods: visit_Import catches 'import x as INDEX_REG', visit_ImportFrom catches non-sm83_model imports that bind a SHARED_TABLE name (via new _is_allowed_sm83_shared_table_import helper that allows both 'from .sm83_model import INDEX_REG' (level=1, module=sm83_model) AND 'from tools.debugger.sm83_model import INDEX_REG' (level=0, absolute)), visit_Attribute catches 'module.INDEX_REG = X' Store-context attribute assignments. Module-scope only (FunctionDef/ClassDef still return early). Live audit PASS, 17 tests pass (was 14, +3). Edges that intentionally remain uncovered (acceptable given the narrow rule): from ..sm83_model imports at level >= 2 (not in this repo's layout), setattr() dynamic mutation, function-local lazy imports. The 'narrow rule' framing in the commit message accurately reflects what shipped — no over-reach into class-locals or runtime introspection.

P5 first slice ready: tools/debugger/context_packet.py (~240 LOC) emits a bug-localization context packet for a single hypothesis id. build_context_packet(hypothesis_id, *, target, max_tokens, root, store) returns kind=unified_debugger_context_packet with markdown + structured JSON + token_count + within_budget + valid + errors fields. Reads from hypothesis_tracker via load_events + fold_history; computes citation drift via detect_citation_drift (mutates folded dict in place, returns reports list — pattern follows the existing API). Token estimate is pessimistic char-based (4 chars per token); will swap for a real tokenizer when budget enforcement becomes load-bearing. Markdown carries: hypothesis id, target reader, status, confidence, citation drift flag, blocked passes, symptom, claim, citations (with stale markers), recent verifications (last 5), notes, and a 'first-slice scaffolding' next-action placeholder. CLI: python -m tools.debugger pack --hypothesis <id> [--target claude|codex] [--max-tokens N] [--json] [--store PATH]. Registered as 9th v2 surface in catalog.py with scope/gaps/commands; pack added to V2_PASSTHROUGH_MODULES in __main__.py so the subcommand bypasses outer argparse cleanly (the same passthrough pattern tdb/when_wrote/handoff use). 5 tests in test_context_packet.py: missing hypothesis returns valid=False, known hypothesis with citations packs claim+citations, token estimate stays within budget for small packets, invalid --target normalizes to claude, CLI --json emission is machine-parseable. test_catalog.py updated for 9/9 v2 surfaces + pack in V2_PASSTHROUGH_MODULES set (pgoal decisions logged for both test edits). NOT YET WIRED: per-LLM punchline-first framing differences (--target codex vs claude currently render identically); taint/slicing spans; effect-trace neighbors; tokenizer-backed budget enforcement; golden lived-bug smoke (AG-08 class). Those land in P5 follow-up slices.
